description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, natural appearance, featuring a mix of earthy brown and striking turquoise-blue mineral deposits across its surface. The stone's texture is rough and uneven, with multiple crevices, pits, and protrusions, giving it a weathered look. Patches of white mineralization are scattered throughout, adding to its visual complexity. The turquoise-blue areas appear to be mineral veins or surface encrustations, possibly indicating the presence of copper minerals such as chrysocolla or turquoise. The stone has a dense, solid structure, with no visible cavities or openings. Small labels or tags are affixed to some surfaces, suggesting it may be part of a collection or study.
description: A rugged, irregular stone with brown, turquoise-blue, and white mineral patterns, featuring rough texture and mineral veins.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broad, uneven top with prominent turquoise-blue and white mineral patches against a brown base.
description_view_1: This angle shows deeper crevices and a more concentrated area of blue mineralization, with a rougher, more jagged profile.
description_view_2: This side reveals a flatter surface with a large central depression and a visible label, emphasizing the stone's layered mineral patterns.
description_view_3: This perspective displays a more vertical face with a mix of blue and brown, and another small label, showing the stone's angular contours.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or decorative obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and mottling of turquoise-blue and white minerals over a brown stone matrix, with no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible mineral encrustations, suggesting prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured or paved urban environments, but can blend with naturalistic landscaping or rock gardens.
